payload.text: Je veux une application hyper simple qui me permet de prendre des notes et de me souvenir chaque jour au standup meeting ce que j'ai fait la veille. On gardera tout dans le local storage, l'utilisateur pourra définir une fenetre glissante de N jours (par défaut les trois derniers). Pas d'IA.

**Conceptualizing the MVP**

I'm focusing now on the Minimum Viable Product. My thoughts are coalescing around a very basic note-taking interface. Daily notes will be the core of it. I'm prioritizing simplicity to get something functional quickly.


**Designing the Interface**

I've sketched out the initial design for the application. A sliding window of the last N days will be the main component, built with React and Tailwind CSS for quality visuals. LocalStorage will handle data persistence, and the entire app will be completely offline. I'm focusing on simplicity and clarity: a prominent editor for today's notes and a card-based layout for past entries. A Slate/Gray color palette with an accent color will keep the focus on the content.


**Refining the Architecture**

I'm now diving into the component structure. I've broken down the design into `index.html`, `types.ts`, `App.tsx`, `NoteEditor.tsx`, and `HistoryView.tsx`. Core state management and the `LocalStorage` sync reside in `App.tsx`. The editor and history views are now separate components with a state passed down to the components. The date calculation, the core for the application, has been thoroughly thought through. I'm focusing on ensuring clear visibility of the most recent non-empty entry and am working on the "Copy for Standup" functionality.

entries.description: App metadata with basic name and description.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Inter font.
React entry point using the createRoot API.
Global type definitions for the application.
Utilities for date manipulation and formatting.
Component to display a single historical note.
Main application component with state management and sliding window logic.

payload.text: Ok ne compte que les jours du lundi au vendredi, enleve les jours fériés. Change le fond de l'input, je veux texte noir sur fond blanc

**Analyzing Weekend Exclusion**

I've been focusing on how to modify the "Standup Memo" application to exclude weekends from the sliding window of previous days. My current line of thinking revolves around implementing a robust date filtering mechanism to achieve this. The challenge lies in ensuring accurate identification of weekends, and proper handling of them during date calculations. I plan to use date-time libraries, but must test for edge cases around the start and end of the relevant reporting window.


**Adding Public Holiday Exclusion**

My focus has shifted to incorporating public holiday exclusion alongside weekend exclusion.  I've decided on a simplified approach for French holidays, hardcoding fixed dates and calculating variable ones like Easter for the current year. This will ensure the "Standup Memo" application can correctly filter for business days, which will enhance date calculations, and I'll include updated styling for the text area to ensure readability.


**Incorporating Business Day Logic**

I'm now implementing the business day logic within the date calculations.  I'm hardcoding French public holidays like Aug 15 (Assomption), Nov 1 (Toussaint), Nov 11 (Armistice), and Dec 25 (Noël). Variable holidays, such as Easter Monday, Ascension, and Pentecost Monday, will be calculated dynamically using a helper function. The `getPreviousDays` function will be modified to use a while loop, decrementing days and only counting business days. I'll also update the text area styling to use `text-black`.

I will update the application to exclude weekends and French public holidays from the history window. I will also adjust the editor's styling to ensure black text on a crisp white background.

### Specification of Updates

1.  **Business Days Logic**:
    *   Modified `getPreviousDays` in `utils/dateUtils.ts` to skip Saturdays and Sundays.
    *   Implemented a French public holiday calculator (Jour de l'an, 1er Mai, 8 Mai, 14 Juillet, 15 Août, 1er Novembre, 11 Novembre, Noël + variable dates like Pâques, Ascension, and Pentecôte).
    *   The "sliding window" of $N$ days will now represent the last $N$ active working days.

2.  **Visual Appearance**:
    *   The main note editor (`textarea`) is updated to have `text-black` on `bg-white` for maximum readability as requested.
    *   Maintained the clean, professional "Inter" font and Tailwind-based layout.

entries.description: Added business day logic and French public holiday calculation.
Updated editor styles for black text on white background and refreshed business day logic integration.

payload.text: On peut sauvegarder le jour actuel et modifier

**Reviewing Current Implementation**

I'm revisiting the current implementation. It seems today's note *is* already automatically saved using `handleTextChange` to `localStorage`. I'm starting to think about how to address the user's desire to edit past notes, given the existing auto-save mechanism. I need to make sure I understand the interaction between `handleTextChange` and localStorage fully.


**Developing the Edit Flow**

I'm now focusing on the history card editability. My current plan is to modify `HistoryCard.tsx` to include an "edit" mode or, even better, have them be textareas by default with minimal styling. This will ensure clicking on a history card enables modification of that day's note, which will then be synced back to the main state in `App.tsx`. I believe this will satisfy the user's need to "modifier" (edit) history.


**Clarifying User Intent**

I'm now revisiting the user's prompt, specifically "modifier." This leads me to believe they want editability, not a separate "save" action. The core focus shifts to direct in-place editing of historical notes. I'm updating `HistoryCard.tsx` to use a `textarea` for direct modification and style it to look like a card. Also, I'll add a "Saving..." indicator for improved feedback during synchronization.


**Integrating History Changes**

I've just added a new `handleHistoryChange` function in `App.tsx` that will update the notes map when history cards are modified. I am also planning to keep the black and white theme and add subtle borders to the editable areas. I am now trying to consider how this function will interact with the textareas.

entries.description: Make history cards editable by replacing the static content with a textarea and adding an onChange callback.
Update App component to handle modifications of both current and past notes, and add a saving indicator for user feedback.

payload.text: Améliore le design avec tailwindcss ou shacdn

**Revising Standup Memo Design**

I'm currently focused on refactoring the "Standup Memo" application's design. The objective is to leverage Tailwind CSS and Headless UI for an improved visual aesthetic and more efficient component management. I'm exploring different layouts and styles to achieve a modern and user-friendly interface.


**Enhancing Note-Taking App**

I'm now focusing on a simple note-taking application designed for tracking daily notes and recalling past entries, excluding weekends and holidays. The key principles are no AI integration, local-only storage, an editable history, and a dark/professional aesthetic. I am leaning into a modern dashboard feel with subtle glassmorphism or high-contrast cards.  I'm also considering Lucide icons for visual cues.  Currently, I am looking to refactor `App.tsx` and focusing on smooth transitions, better hover states, and maybe a "Copy" icon instead of just text.


**Optimizing UI Elements**

I'm now revising the design of the note-taking application. "Aujourd'hui" is getting a workspace upgrade and "Historique" now has improved date markers and spacing. I'm focusing on "HistoryCard.tsx" with an improved border and auto-resizing textarea. I've added a blur effect to the header, a background gradient to the main container, and styled the buttons with consistent shapes and colors, with a status indicator for saving. Shadcn's minimalist style is guiding manual recreation of look.  Finally, I'm ensuring I don't disrupt the existing logic for business days and holidays.


**Improving App Aesthetics**

I'm now implementing the visual updates. The sidebar will feature a subtle left border, and I'm giving the main editor a slightly raised, "sheet" appearance.  I'm adding SVG icons for "Copy," "Calendar," and "Save" for clarity.  I'm also updating `App.tsx` and `HistoryCard.tsx` to integrate these changes into the app's structure and overall style. Finally, I'll update `index.html` to add the Lucide icons.

Pour améliorer le design vers une esthétique "Modern SaaS / Shadcn", je vais affiner la typographie, les ombres, les bordures et les états d'interaction. Nous allons utiliser des couleurs plus nuancées (Slat-900 pour le texte, Slate-500 pour les secondaires) et ajouter des icônes SVG pour une meilleure affordance.

### Spécifications du nouveau design :

1.  **Typographie & Couleurs** : Utilisation d'un contraste élevé (Noir pur sur blanc) avec des accents de Slate pour les bordures et les fonds secondaires.
2.  **Composants "Shadcn-like"** :
    *   **Cartes** : Bordures fines (`border-slate-200`), coins arrondis généreux (`rounded-xl`), et ombres portées très légères.
    *   **Inputs** : Focus avec un anneau de décalage (`ring-offset-2`) pour un aspect professionnel.
    *   **Header** : Effet de flou (`backdrop-blur-md`) pour une sensation de profondeur.
3.  **Interaction** :
    *   Ajout d'une animation "pulse" discrète lors de la sauvegarde.
    *   Amélioration de la zone d'édition pour qu'elle ressemble à une "page" de carnet.
    *   Utilisation d'icônes (SVG) pour les actions principales (Copier, Calendrier, Paramètres).
4.  **Layout** : Structure plus aérée avec des espacements standardisés (`gap-8`, `p-6`).

entries.description: Refonte visuelle de la carte d'historique avec un style minimaliste et une zone de texte mieux intégrée.
Refonte de l'interface principale avec un style moderne, des icônes et une meilleure hiérarchie visuelle.

payload.text: Mets les suggestions / placeholder en noir

**Adjusting the Color**

I'm now thinking specifically about how the placeholder text looks. I've been working in `App.tsx` and have a placeholder that's currently a light grey. My next move is to make the placeholder black, to improve its visibility for the user. I'll need to update the style associated with the `textarea` element.


**Prioritizing Placeholder Contrast**

I've been examining the placeholder text styles in `App.tsx` and `HistoryCard.tsx`. While the initial instinct was to make it black per the user request, I'm now reconsidering. Black can be too intense and potentially confusing because it can resemble typed text. I'll probably go with a very dark grey, like `text-slate-900`, or add opacity to `text-black` to preserve distinction. My focus is still on ensuring sufficient contrast.


**Finalizing Placeholder Appearance**

I'm now going to make the placeholder black, as the user requested. Although I prefer a dark gray, the user is the priority. I'll make the updates in `App.tsx` and `components/HistoryCard.tsx`. I should also review `index.html` for any potential conflicts, but Tailwind classes will be my primary method of change.

entries.description: Mise à jour de la couleur du placeholder en noir pour l'éditeur principal.
Mise à jour de la couleur du placeholder en noir pour les cartes d'historique.

payload.text:
Je veux faire deux types de changement.
Premièrement le texte d'aujourd'hui et quasi autant important que celui d'hier, car cela me permet de me souvenir quoi dire au standup.
Le reste des jours sont des archives.

Ensuite je veux définir ou non des todolist sur une journée. Les items non cochés seront alors automatiquement reporté au jour d'après, sauf si je décide de les effacer le lendemain

**Defining Key Standup Elements**

I'm now zeroing in on the core features for a standup note-taking app. My primary focus is making "Yesterday" and "Today" the immediate focal points. The app needs to make it extremely easy to capture "what I did yesterday", which is crucial for the standup format. I'm prioritizing minimal friction for rapid input.


**Integrating Daily Tasks and Archives**

I've been drilling down on the data structure. I'm updating `NotesMap` to include both text content and a `todos` array. Each `Todo` object will track ID, text, completion status, and a `rolledFrom` date (if applicable). This will enable automated task rollover from the previous day to today. The UI will now highlight today and yesterday. I am adding a to-do section within the daily editor and introducing an "Archives" section. Rollover logic is being implemented.


**Clarifying Date-Handling Mechanics**

I'm now focusing on date handling. The key is to define "Hier" precisely. I'm prioritizing data structure refinement, specifically in `types.ts`. I anticipate needing a helper function in `utils/dateUtils.ts` to identify the last business day, which will be essential for the rollover logic. I'm also planning to incorporate a Shadcn-like component for UI, to better visually define "Hier".


**Refining App Architecture**

I'm now implementing the core functionality and I'm updating `types.ts` with `Todo` and `DailyData` interfaces and `NotesMap` type. The app logic in `App.tsx` focuses on retrieving and displaying "yesterday" and "today" data, also using `useEffect` for loading and initialization. I'm focusing on task rollover using logic to prevent duplicates. I'm also preparing to refactor `HistoryCard.tsx` to serve as an "ArchiveCard".

entries.description: Update data structures to support to-dos and structured daily data.
Implement Today/Yesterday focused layout with automatic to-do rollover logic and modernized UI.
Simplify HistoryCard to act as an archive display while maintaining editability.

payload.text: Attention je veux du texte noir sur des fonds blancs

**Emphasizing Standup Prep**

I'm focused on the user's desire to highlight "Today" and "Yesterday" as the core elements for standup preparation. It seems clear that both are equally crucial, and this should be reflected in the app's structure. I'm exploring how to best emphasize these two key periods.


**Developing The Rollover**

I'm now fully immersed in the "rollover" logic. It's becoming apparent that my initial approach to handling unfinished tasks from "Yesterday" is on the right track. I'm prioritizing the implementation of a to-do list for each day and the automatic transfer of incomplete items. My focus has shifted toward refining the precise algorithm for this process, ensuring that the app seamlessly manages the rollover, allowing for deletion of items that aren't relevant to today.


**Implementing Data Structure**

I'm now deep in defining the data structure within `types.ts`. I'm creating `notes[today]` with its associated to-do list and empty notes field. Saving functionality is the next priority. I am considering the optimal UI layout, featuring two side-by-side cards for Today and Yesterday, each with a Note area and To-do area. Backgrounds and text colors are also a major consideration.

C'est entendu. Je vais réorganiser l'application pour mettre l'accent sur le duo **Aujourd'hui / Hier** (indispensable pour le standup) et implémenter le **report automatique des tâches non terminées**.

Le design sera strictement **texte noir sur fond blanc**, avec une esthétique minimaliste et professionnelle.

### Spécifications des changements :

1.  **Layout "Split View"** : Aujourd'hui et Hier s'affichent côte à côte (ou l'un sur l'autre sur mobile) avec une importance égale.
2.  **To-Do List intelligente** :
    *   Chaque jour possède son bloc de notes et sa liste de tâches.
    *   Au premier chargement de la journée, l'application vérifie le dernier jour ouvré.
    *   Toutes les tâches **non cochées** sont automatiquement créées dans la liste d'aujourd'hui.
    *   Possibilité de supprimer une tâche (même reportée) d'un clic.
3.  **Archives** : Les jours précédents sont relégués dans une section "Archives" plus discrète en bas de page.
4.  **Contraste Maximum** : Fond blanc (`bg-white`), texte noir (`text-black`), bordures fines (`border-slate-200`).

entries.description: Mise à jour des types pour inclure les Todos et la structure de données quotidienne.
Simplification de la carte d'archive pour un style minimaliste noir sur blanc.
Refonte complète du layout avec focus Hier/Aujourd'hui, logique de report de tâches et design noir sur blanc.
